task_context.question: Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. and what other South Korean comprise a group created in 2015?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. and what other South Korean comprise a group created in 2015?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Monsta X — formation and membership (2015)

- Passage [1]: "Monsta X ... is a South Korean boy group formed ... in 2015" and formed by "Starship Entertainment" via survival show "'No.Mercy' on Mnet".
- Passage [1] lists the group's members: "composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M."
- Ambiguities: "Wonho" appears also as "Wonho Chung" in passage [2] (different person); "Minhyuk" appears as "Lee Min-hyuk" of BtoB in passage [3] (different person). Label these as name-variant ambiguities.
- Next-hop retrieval (priority): 1) No additional retrieval needed (passage [1] contains the full member list). 2) If verification of individual biographies is desired, fetch the "Monsta X" page lede/infobox and the individual member pages/sections (e.g., "Members" or each member's article). 3) Optionally fetch disambiguation or biography pages for "Wonho" and "Minhyuk" to resolve name-variant ambiguity.

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Monsta X | Monsta X (Hangul: 몬스타엑스; often stylized as MONSTA X) is a South Korean boy group formed by Starship Entertainment through the survival show "No.Mercy" on Mnet in 2015. The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M.

Monsta Island Czars discography | This is the solo and group discography of the legendary underground hip hop collective Monsta Island Czars. Included are the solo studio albums, EPs and compilations of the various group members and affiliates. Although Monsta Island Czars aren't formed until after the death of Subroc, KMD's albums Mr. Hood and Black Bastards are stylistic precursors to what will appear in the Monsta Island Czars catalog.
Monsta Island Czars | Monsta Island Czars is a hip-hop collective based in New York City, New York, United States most notable for members MF Grimm and MF Doom.

Escape from Monsta Island! | Escape from Monsta Island! is a studio album released by the Monsta Island Czars, a collective notably featuring MF Doom under his King Geedorah alias. Although the Monsta Island Czars was founded by MF Grimm, he is absent on the entire album due to his incarceration at the time of its production and release.

Monsta X discography | The South Korean hip-hop boy group Monsta X, produced and managed by Starship Entertainment debuted in 2015 and have currently released one studio album, four extended plays, one compilation album, one video album, over ten music videos and eleven singles. The group and members have also participated in singing nine soundtracks for various Korean dramas.

Monsta (EP) | Monsta is the debut extended play (EP) by the British band I See MONSTAS (credited as MONSTA on the release). It was released on 23 October 2012 through OWSLA. The EP spawned a single, "Messiah", which entered the UK Singles Chart at number 154. "Holdin' On" climbed the UK Singles Chart to number 48, due to strong sales of the Skrillex and Nero remix. It also peaked at number ten on UK Dance Chart and number three the UK Indie Chart.

I See Monstas | I See Monstas (often stylised I See MONSTAS, and previously called MONSTA), are a British electronic music group. The group was formed in 2012 and consists of producers Rocky and Rufio (also known collectively as Pegasus) as well as singer Bryn Christopher (also known as Skaar). The trio have officially remixed Labrinth ("Last Time"), Zedd ("Spectrum"), and Tinie Tempah ("Trampoline") among others. They have also produced for Ellie Goulding the songs "Figure 8" and "Ritual" from  ...[truncated]
source_state.summary_2: The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. Monsta X was formed by Starship Entertainment in 2015 through the Mnet survival show "No.Mercy".

output.query: Lee Joo-heon Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. and what other South Korean comprise a group created in 2015?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Monsta X | Monsta X (Hangul: 몬스타엑스; often stylized as MONSTA X) is a South Korean boy group formed by Starship Entertainment through the survival show "No.Mercy" on Mnet in 2015. The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M.

Lee Joo-heon | Lee Joo-heon (born Lee Ho-joon on October 6, 1994), better known by the mononym Jooheon, is a South Korean rapper and songwriter. He is a member of South Korean boy group Monsta X, formed during the 2014 Mnet's survival show "No.Mercy".

Lee Min-hyuk | Lee Min-hyuk (; born November 29, 1990), commonly known as Minhyuk or Heota, is a South Korean singer, rapper, songwriter and actor. He is a member of the South Korean boy group BtoB. He received multiple supporting roles in television series such as "A New Leaf" and "Sweet, Savage Family".
Lee Dae-heon | Lee Dae-heon (born 17 November 1993) is a South Korean professional footballer.
Lee Byeong-heon (filmmaker) | Lee Byeong-heon (; born 23 July 1980) is a South Korean film director and screenwriter. He is best known for his 2014 film "Twenty".

Twenty (film) | Twenty () is a 2015 South Korean coming-of-age film starring Kim Woo-bin, Lee Junho and Kang Ha-neul. It was written and directed by Lee Byeong-heon, his second feature after the 2012 indie "Cheer Up, Mr. Lee".
Lee Sang-heon | Lee Sang-heon () is a Korean name consisting of the family name Lee and the given name Sang-heon, and may also refer to:
target_state.summary_2: The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. Lee Joo-heon is a South Korean rapper and songwriter better known by the mononym Jooheon and is a member of Monsta X.
